stem: A education study measures average test score improvement across 4 schools, each containing multiple classrooms. school A has 20 observations with mean 78.3 points (within-school SD = 7.9). school B has 10 observations with mean 95.9 points (within-school SD = 7.2). school C has 15 observations with mean 79.3 points (within-school SD = 9.3). school D has 20 observations with mean 88.8 points (within-school SD = 6.1). The between-school standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 5.2 and 13.0 points. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true average test score improvement for school D, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-school variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for school D: 88.7 points (shrunk from raw 88.8). Range [88.5, 88.8] as between-school SD varies from 5.2 to 13.0. Pooled mean: 84.5.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 84.5 points (weighted average of 4 school means).
Shrinkage factor for school D: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/20). At σ_between=8.7: B = 0.976.
Interval [88.5, 88.8] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (88.8) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (84.5) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
